tasks.title: Build full PII/PHI scanner, reduction, migration, and retention platform
tasks.description: Turn ScanMeNow from a Phileas-derived redaction library into a full data governance engine for discovering, reducing, migrating, and retaining sensitive data. Parent epic for all Phase 1-7 subtasks.

tasks.title: Group A - 1.1 - Python project skeleton (uv init, src layout, pyproject.toml, tests/)
tasks.description: Initialize the Python project with uv, establish src/ layout, configure pyproject.toml with project metadata and tool settings, create tests/ directory with pytest config.

tasks.title: Group A - 1.2 - Typer CLI scaffold (entry point, --help works, smoke test)
tasks.description: Create the scanmenow CLI entry point using Typer. Wire pyproject.toml scripts. Verify --help outputs correctly. Add a smoke test that invokes the CLI and asserts exit code 0.

tasks.title: Group A - 1.3 - Presidio Analyzer integration (load + run on sample text + smoke test)
tasks.description: Install and configure microsoft/presidio-analyzer. Load the default engine. Run it against a sample text string containing a name and SSN. Assert findings are returned. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group A - 1.4 - Presidio Anonymizer integration (redact sample text + smoke test)
tasks.description: Install presidio-anonymizer. Wire it to the Analyzer output. Redact sample text and assert sensitive spans are replaced. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group A - 1.5 - spaCy NER model bootstrap (download en_core_web_lg, verify outputs)
tasks.description: Add en_core_web_lg as a project dependency or download step. Verify it loads without error and produces NER tags on sample text. Add a fixture and smoke test.

tasks.title: Group B - 1.6 - Gap analysis: 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers vs Presidio built-ins (checklist)
tasks.description: Enumerate all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers. Map each to a Presidio built-in recognizer or flag as missing. Produce a gap checklist committed to docs/. This drives the porting tranches.

tasks.title: Group B - 1.7 - Port phileas rules Tranche A: names, SSN, MRN, DOB (recognizers + unit tests)
tasks.description: Read phileas Java recognizer source for names, SSN, MRN, and DOB. Port regex patterns and context words to Python Presidio PatternRecognizer subclasses. Add unit tests per identifier against known fixtures.

tasks.title: Group B - 1.8 - Port phileas rules Tranche B: phone, email, address, account # (recognizers + unit tests)
tasks.description: Port phone, email, address, and account number recognizers from phileas to Python PatternRecognizer subclasses. Add unit tests per identifier.

tasks.title: Group B - 1.9 - Port phileas rules Tranche C: device IDs, plan beneficiary, biometric, IP/URL (recognizers + unit tests)
tasks.description: Port device identifiers, health plan beneficiary number, biometric identifiers, and IP/URL recognizers from phileas. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group C - 1.10 - Synthetic PHI corpus + golden findings JSON across all 18 identifiers
tasks.description: Create a synthetic PHI test corpus covering all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers. For each document, produce a golden findings JSON with expected entity type, span start/end, and confidence floor. Commit to tests/corpus/.

tasks.title: Group C - 1.11 - Accuracy harness (corpus to diff to precision/recall report per identifier)
tasks.description: Build a test harness that runs the Presidio engine against the synthetic corpus and diffs results against golden findings. Output precision and recall per identifier. Fail if below configurable thresholds.

tasks.title: Group C - 1.12 - Threshold tuning per identifier (Presidio confidence calibration)
tasks.description: Run accuracy harness and tune Presidio score thresholds per identifier to meet precision/recall targets. Document thresholds in a config file. Commit tuned config.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.13 - Local filesystem walker (path resolution, skip patterns, symlink policy, max-depth)
tasks.description: Implement a filesystem walker that resolves absolute paths, respects configurable skip-pattern globs, follows or skips symlinks per policy, and enforces max-depth. Returns an iterator of file paths.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.14 - File-type detection + dispatcher (extension + magic-byte fallback)
tasks.description: Detect file type by extension first, fall back to magic-byte sniffing via python-magic. Dispatch each file to the appropriate scanner. Return unsupported-type findings for unknown formats.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.15 - TXT scanner
tasks.description: Implement a plain-text scanner that reads a file, runs the Presidio engine over it in chunks, and returns structured findings with character offsets. Add unit test against fixture.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.16 - CSV scanner (row/column-aware findings)
tasks.description: Implement a CSV scanner that reads each row, runs detection per cell, and returns findings with row index and column name in the location metadata. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.17 - JSON scanner (jsonpath-aware findings)
tasks.description: Implement a JSON scanner that traverses all string values, runs detection, and returns findings with JSONPath location. Handle nested structures and arrays. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group D - 1.18 - PDF text-layer scanner (PyMuPDF)
tasks.description: Implement a PDF scanner using PyMuPDF to extract text layer per page. Run detection per page. Return findings with page number and character offset. Add unit test against fixture PDF.

tasks.title: Group E - 1.19 - Firestore connection layer (env flag to disable cloud writes for local dev)
tasks.description: Set up google-cloud-firestore client. Load credentials from GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS. Add SCANMENOW_LOCAL_ONLY env flag that disables all Firestore writes for local dev. Add connection smoke test.

tasks.title: Group E - 1.20 - Findings data model (job, finding, safe-evidence snippet, raw-disabled default)
tasks.description: Define Pydantic models for ScanJob, Finding, Span, and SafeEvidence. Raw sensitive values disabled by default; safe evidence shows surrounding context with value masked. Include serialization to/from Firestore document format.

tasks.title: Group E - 1.21 - Job persistence + idempotency (resume from checkpoint, dedupe by file hash)
tasks.description: Persist scan jobs and per-file progress to Firestore. On resume, skip files already processed (matched by content hash). Mark job status as running/complete/failed. Add integration test with local-only flag.

tasks.title: Group E - 1.22 - End-to-end CLI: scanmenow scan ./path --policy <file>
tasks.description: Wire walker + dispatcher + scanners + policy + Firestore into the scanmenow scan command. Accept a directory path and optional policy file. Output JSON and CSV findings reports. Add e2e test against a fixture directory.

tasks.title: Group F - 1.23 - Phase 1 exit gate: accuracy >= threshold + CLI works on real fixture directory
tasks.description: Run accuracy harness against full corpus. Verify all 18 identifiers meet precision/recall thresholds. Run CLI end-to-end against a real fixture directory. Document results. Gate blocks Phase 2 start.

tasks.title: Group A - 2.1 - Policy schema (YAML/JSON format, version field, action definitions)
tasks.description: Design and document the reduction policy schema: YAML/JSON format, schema version field, list of rules each with entity-type matcher, confidence threshold, and action name + parameters. Commit schema spec and JSON Schema validator.

tasks.title: Group A - 2.2 - Policy loader + validator (parse, schema-validate, error messages)
tasks.description: Implement policy file loader that reads YAML or JSON, validates against the JSON Schema, and returns typed Policy objects. Produce human-readable error messages on invalid policy. Add unit tests with valid and invalid fixtures.

tasks.title: Group A - 2.3 - Policy engine core (match findings to policy rules, select action)
tasks.description: Implement the policy engine that takes a list of findings and a loaded policy, matches each finding to the first matching rule by entity type and confidence, and returns an action plan. Add unit tests covering rule precedence and no-match fallback.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.4 - Redact action (replace with [REDACTED] or custom label)
tasks.description: Implement the redact action: replace a matched span with [REDACTED] or a configurable label. Mark as irreversible. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.5 - Mask action (partial masking, e.g. last 4 of SSN)
tasks.description: Implement the mask action: reveal a configurable prefix or suffix of the matched value and replace the rest with a mask character. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.6 - Hash action (SHA-256, configurable algorithm)
tasks.description: Implement the hash action: replace matched span with hex digest of the value. Algorithm configurable (default SHA-256). Mark as irreversible. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.7 - Tokenize action (replace with stable pseudonym, lookup table)
tasks.description: Implement tokenization: replace matched value with a stable synthetic token stored in a lookup table (Firestore-backed). Same value always maps to same token within a job. Reversible via lookup. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.8 - Encrypt action (AES-256-GCM, key ref via env var)
tasks.description: Implement encryption action using AES-256-GCM. Key loaded from env var reference in policy. Output is base64-encoded ciphertext + nonce. Reversible with key. Mark as requiring key management. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.9 - Anonymize action (Presidio-backed generalization)
tasks.description: Implement anonymize action using Presidio Anonymizer operators (replace, fake, etc.). Configurable operator per entity type. Mark as irreversible for most operators. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.10 - Bucket action (age ranges, zip-code generalization)
tasks.description: Implement bucketing action: replace numeric or geographic values with a range/bucket (e.g., age 34 to 30-39, zip 10001 to 100**). Configurable bucket definitions. Mark as irreversible. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.11 - Drop action (remove entire field/row)
tasks.description: Implement drop action: remove the entire field (JSON key), CSV row, or text line containing the matched span. Mark as irreversible. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 2.12 - Quarantine action (move file/record to quarantine location)
tasks.description: Implement quarantine action: move the source file or record to a configured quarantine directory/collection. Log original path. Mark as reversible (can restore). Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 2.13 - Dry-run mode (diff output, no writes, shows what would change)
tasks.description: Implement dry-run execution mode: run the policy engine and actions but make no file writes. Output a diff showing original vs. would-be-reduced for each file. Add flag to CLI and unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 2.14 - Reduction executor (apply actions to local files, write to output dir)
tasks.description: Implement the reduction executor: read source files, apply action plan from policy engine, write transformed output to a configured output directory. Preserve directory structure. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group C - 2.15 - Diff report (before/after, findings resolved, actions taken)
tasks.description: Generate a structured diff report after reduction: list each finding, the action taken, and before/after evidence snippets. Output as JSON and human-readable text. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 2.16 - Irreversible-action warnings + approval gate
tasks.description: Before executing any irreversible action (redact, hash, drop, anonymize, bucket), print a warning listing affected files and finding counts. Require explicit --confirm flag or interactive prompt to proceed. Add test.

tasks.title: Group D - 2.17 - Reduction audit events (action taken, finding ID, timestamp, user)
tasks.description: Write an audit event to Firestore for every reduction action executed: finding ID, action type, timestamp, source path, user identity. Add unit test with local-only flag.

tasks.title: Group D - 2.18 - CLI: scanmenow reduce --mode dry-run + scanmenow reduce --out <dir>
tasks.description: Wire reduction executor, dry-run mode, diff report, warnings, and audit into the scanmenow reduce CLI command. Add e2e test against a fixture directory.

tasks.title: Group A - 3.1 - Connector interface (read_stream, list_objects, metadata protocol)
tasks.description: Define the abstract Connector base class with protocol methods: list_objects(), read_stream(ref), write_stream(ref, stream), delete(ref), and get_metadata(ref). Document the contract. Add ABC enforcement.

tasks.title: Group A - 3.2 - Connector registry (discover + load connectors by type)
tasks.description: Implement a connector registry that maps source type strings (local, s3, gcs, azure, postgres, mysql, sqlserver) to connector classes. Support dynamic registration. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 3.3 - Local filesystem connector (refactor walker into connector abstraction)
tasks.description: Refactor the Phase 1 filesystem walker into a LocalConnector implementing the Connector interface. Preserve all walker behavior. Add unit test against fixture directory.

tasks.title: Group B - 3.4 - S3-compatible connector (boto3, AWS S3 + MinIO)
tasks.description: Implement S3Connector using boto3. Support AWS S3 and MinIO via endpoint_url override. Implement list_objects, read_stream, write_stream, delete, get_metadata. Add unit test with moto mock.

tasks.title: Group B - 3.5 - GCS connector (google-cloud-storage)
tasks.description: Implement GCSConnector using google-cloud-storage. Implement full Connector interface. Add unit test with mock client.

tasks.title: Group B - 3.6 - Azure Blob Storage connector
tasks.description: Implement AzureBlobConnector using azure-storage-blob. Implement full Connector interface. Add unit test with mock client.

tasks.title: Group C - 3.7 - Postgres connector (psycopg3, schema discovery, row streaming)
tasks.description: Implement PostgresConnector using psycopg3. Support schema discovery (list tables and columns). Stream rows as dicts. Implement Connector interface. Add integration test with dockerized Postgres fixture.

tasks.title: Group C - 3.8 - MySQL connector (mysql-connector-python, schema discovery)
tasks.description: Implement MySQLConnector using mysql-connector-python. Schema discovery and row streaming. Add integration test with dockerized MySQL fixture.

tasks.title: Group C - 3.9 - SQL Server connector (pyodbc, schema discovery)
tasks.description: Implement SQLServerConnector using pyodbc. Schema discovery and row streaming. Add integration test with dockerized SQL Server fixture.

tasks.title: Group C - 3.10 - JDBC fallback connector (jaydebeapi, for other RDBMS)
tasks.description: Implement JDBCConnector using jaydebeapi as a fallback for RDBMS not covered by native connectors. Accept JDBC URL, driver class, and JAR path. Add unit test with H2 in-memory database.

tasks.title: Group D - 3.11 - Connector test harness (mock + live fixtures per connector)
tasks.description: Build a shared test harness for connectors: mock implementations for unit tests and live fixture configs for integration tests. Validate all connectors against the interface contract. Add parametrized test suite.

tasks.title: Group D - 3.12 - Scanner integration: wire connectors into scan pipeline
tasks.description: Update the scan pipeline to accept a Connector instead of a raw path. Route object streams through the file-type dispatcher and scanners. Support all implemented connectors. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group D - 3.13 - CLI: scanmenow scan --source s3://bucket/path
tasks.description: Extend the scanmenow scan CLI to accept a --source flag with a URI scheme (local://, s3://, gcs://, postgres://, etc.). Resolve the appropriate connector and run the scan. Add e2e test.

tasks.title: Group A - 4.1 - Migration job schema (source, destination, transform policy, checkpoint)
tasks.description: Define the MigrationJob data model: source connector ref, destination connector ref, optional transform policy, checkpoint field, status, and lineage ID. Commit schema and Pydantic models.

tasks.title: Group A - 4.2 - Lineage model (source record to destination record to transformation applied)
tasks.description: Define the Lineage data model: source ref, destination ref, transformation applied (action + policy version), timestamp, and job ID. Persist to Firestore. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group A - 4.3 - Checkpoint + resume (idempotent migration, mark processed records)
tasks.description: Implement checkpoint storage for migration jobs. Track which source refs have been successfully migrated. On resume, skip completed refs. Add unit test covering resume behavior.

tasks.title: Group B - 4.4 - File-to-file migration (local to local, local to S3, S3 to GCS, etc.)
tasks.description: Implement file-to-file migration: stream objects from source connector, apply optional transform, write to destination connector. Preserve metadata. Record lineage. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group B - 4.5 - Database-to-database migration (Postgres to Postgres, MySQL to SQL Server)
tasks.description: Implement DB-to-DB migration: stream rows from source DB connector, apply optional transform, insert into destination DB connector. Handle schema mapping. Record lineage. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group B - 4.6 - Database-to-object-store migration
tasks.description: Implement DB-to-object-store migration: export rows as CSV/JSON, stream to object store connector. Apply transform. Record lineage. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group B - 4.7 - Object-store-to-object-store migration
tasks.description: Implement object-store-to-object-store migration (e.g., S3 to GCS, GCS to Azure). Stream blobs, apply optional transform, write to destination. Record lineage. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group C - 4.8 - Transform pipeline (apply reduction actions mid-migration)
tasks.description: Wire the reduction policy engine into the migration pipeline. Scan each object/row mid-flight, apply action plan, write transformed output to destination. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group C - 4.9 - Validation step (post-migration record count, hash verification)
tasks.description: After migration, verify record count at destination matches source. For files, verify SHA-256 hash (pre-transform). Produce a validation report. Fail migration job on mismatch. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group D - 4.10 - Approval gate for destructive migrations (require explicit confirm)
tasks.description: Before executing any migration that transforms or deletes source data, require an explicit --confirm flag or interactive approval. Log the approval event. Add test.

tasks.title: Group D - 4.11 - Rollback plan (where reversible; flag where not)
tasks.description: For each migration path, document and implement rollback behavior where feasible (delete destination objects). Flag irreversible paths (destructive transforms, delete-source). Surface rollback status in the job model.

tasks.title: Group D - 4.12 - Migration audit events (lineage record per migrated item)
tasks.description: Write a Firestore audit event for every migrated object/row: source ref, destination ref, transform applied, timestamp, job ID. Add unit test with local-only flag.

tasks.title: Group D - 4.13 - CLI: scanmenow migrate --source --dest --policy
tasks.description: Wire migration engine into the CLI. Accept --source URI, --dest URI, optional --policy file, --dry-run flag, and --confirm flag. Add e2e test.

tasks.title: Group A - 5.1 - Retention policy schema (window, action, triggers, legal hold flag)
tasks.description: Define the retention policy schema: retention window (duration or date), lifecycle action (delete/archive/anonymize), trigger conditions, and legal hold flag. Commit schema spec and Pydantic models.

tasks.title: Group A - 5.2 - Legal hold model (hold ID, scope, reason, release approval)
tasks.description: Define the LegalHold model: hold ID, scope (job IDs or data source refs), reason, held-by user, held-at timestamp, and release approval workflow. Persist to Firestore. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group A - 5.3 - Retention policy loader + validator
tasks.description: Implement a loader that reads retention policy YAML/JSON, validates against schema, and returns typed RetentionPolicy objects. Produce human-readable errors on invalid policy. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group B - 5.4 - Expiration + delete action (with irreversible warning + audit)
tasks.description: Implement expiration-triggered delete: identify findings/jobs past retention window, warn user, require approval, execute deletion via connector, write audit event. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 5.5 - Archive action (move to cold storage connector)
tasks.description: Implement archive action: move data from active connector to a configured cold storage connector (e.g., S3 Glacier, GCS Archive). Preserve lineage. Write audit event. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group B - 5.6 - Anonymize-on-expire action (apply anonymization at retention boundary)
tasks.description: Implement anonymize-on-expire: when a record hits its retention window, apply the anonymize reduction action in place rather than deleting. Mark as irreversible. Write audit event. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 5.7 - Simulation mode (show what would be retained/archived/deleted)
tasks.description: Implement retention simulation: evaluate all policies against the findings store without executing any actions. Output a report showing what would be retained, archived, anonymized, or deleted. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 5.8 - Approval gate for destructive retention actions
tasks.description: Before executing delete or irreversible anonymize retention actions, require explicit --confirm flag or interactive approval. Check for active legal holds and block if hold covers the target data. Log approval event.

tasks.title: Group C - 5.9 - Retention audit events (policy matched, action taken, hold status)
tasks.description: Write Firestore audit events for all retention engine actions: policy matched, action type, target ref, hold status, approval, and timestamp. Add unit test with local-only flag.

tasks.title: Group D - 5.10 - Retention engine executor (evaluate policies against findings store)
tasks.description: Implement the retention engine: load all active retention policies, query findings store for data past retention windows, apply lifecycle actions in order (holds checked first, then delete/archive/anonymize). Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group D - 5.11 - CLI: scanmenow retain --simulate + scanmenow retain --run
tasks.description: Wire retention engine into CLI. Implement scanmenow retain --simulate (dry-run report) and scanmenow retain --run (execute with approval gate). Add e2e test.

tasks.title: Group A - 6.1 - FastAPI app skeleton (routes, middleware, error handlers)
tasks.description: Initialize the FastAPI application. Configure CORS, request logging middleware, and a global error handler that does not leak internal details. Register route prefixes for all resource groups. Add smoke test.

tasks.title: Group A - 6.2 - Auth layer (API key + JWT, per-endpoint enforcement)
tasks.description: Implement API authentication: API key validation via header and JWT bearer token support. Enforce auth on all non-health endpoints via FastAPI dependency injection. Add unit tests for auth pass and fail cases.

tasks.title: Group A - 6.3 - OpenAPI spec + auto-docs
tasks.description: Configure FastAPI OpenAPI metadata: title, version, description, contact, license. Verify /docs and /openapi.json render correctly. Add request/response schemas for all routes. Add test.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.4 - Scan jobs API (POST /jobs, GET /jobs/{id}, GET /jobs/{id}/findings)
tasks.description: Implement scan job REST endpoints: create a job, get job status, and list findings for a job. Wire to Firestore job persistence. Add unit tests with mock Firestore.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.5 - Sources API (CRUD for data source definitions)
tasks.description: Implement data source CRUD endpoints: create, read, update, delete source definitions (type + connection config). Store in Firestore. Validate connector type on create. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.6 - Policies API (CRUD + versioning for reduction/retention policies)
tasks.description: Implement policy CRUD endpoints with policy versioning: each update creates a new version, prior versions are retained. Serve active version by default. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.7 - Findings API (query, filter, export CSV/JSON)
tasks.description: Implement findings query endpoints: filter by job ID, entity type, confidence range, and source path. Support CSV and JSON export formats. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.8 - Migrations API (POST /migrations, GET /migrations/{id}, approve)
tasks.description: Implement migration REST endpoints: create job, get status with lineage summary, and an approve endpoint for destructive jobs requiring confirmation. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group B - 6.9 - Retention rules API (CRUD + simulate endpoint)
tasks.description: Implement retention rule CRUD endpoints and a POST /retention/simulate endpoint that runs the retention engine in simulation mode and returns the report. Add unit tests.

tasks.title: Group C - 6.10 - Worker process (consume job queue, execute scans/reductions/migrations)
tasks.description: Implement an async worker process that polls the job queue, executes scan/reduction/migration jobs, updates job status, and handles failures with retry. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group C - 6.11 - Job queue (Firestore-backed task queue)
tasks.description: Implement a Firestore-backed job queue: enqueue jobs, claim with optimistic locking, heartbeat, and release on completion or failure. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group C - 6.12 - Retry + backoff + dead-letter handling
tasks.description: Add retry logic with exponential backoff to the worker. After max retries, move the job to a dead-letter collection and alert. Add unit test.

tasks.title: Group D - 6.13 - UI scaffold (lightweight HTML dashboard, HTMX or React)
tasks.description: Scaffold the web UI: choose HTMX + Jinja2 (served via FastAPI) or a separate React SPA. Set up routing, layout, and a placeholder home page. Add smoke test.

tasks.title: Group D - 6.14 - Scans + findings view
tasks.description: Build the scan jobs list and detail views: show job status, progress, and a paginated findings table filterable by entity type and confidence. Wire to the Findings API.

tasks.title: Group D - 6.15 - Policy management UI
tasks.description: Build the policy management views: list policies, create/edit YAML policy with inline validation, view version history. Wire to Policies API.

tasks.title: Group D - 6.16 - Migration + retention status UI
tasks.description: Build migration job list/detail views (with lineage and approval button for pending jobs) and retention simulation view. Wire to Migrations API and Retention rules API.

tasks.title: Group A - 7.1 - Security audit (OWASP Top 10, input validation, auth bypass checks)
tasks.description: Run a full security audit of the API and CLI: check for injection vulnerabilities, auth bypass, insecure deserialization, sensitive data exposure, and OWASP Top 10. Fix all critical and high findings before v1.0.

tasks.title: Group A - 7.2 - Key management design (KMS integration for encrypt/FPE actions)
tasks.description: Design and implement key management for the encrypt action: integrate with a KMS (Google Cloud KMS or AWS KMS). Keys never stored in config or env vars. Document key rotation procedure. Add integration test.

tasks.title: Group A - 7.3 - HIPAA BAA documentation + Firestore config checklist
tasks.description: Document the HIPAA BAA requirement for Google Cloud Firestore when storing PHI metadata. Produce a deployment checklist: sign BAA, configure Firestore in a compliant region, enable audit logging, restrict access. Commit to docs/.

tasks.title: Group B - 7.4 - Evaluation corpus expansion (broader synthetic PHI + edge cases)
tasks.description: Expand the synthetic PHI corpus with edge cases: multi-language names, ambiguous identifiers, near-miss values, mixed formats, and long documents. Update golden findings. Rerun accuracy harness.

tasks.title: Group B - 7.5 - Accuracy regression CI gate (precision/recall must not regress)
tasks.description: Wire the accuracy harness into CI so every PR must pass the precision/recall thresholds on the full expanded corpus. Fail the build on regression. Add a badge to README.

tasks.title: Group C - 7.6 - Install + user docs (README, quickstart, policy reference)
tasks.description: Write the user-facing README: installation via pip/uv, quickstart for scanmenow scan and scanmenow reduce, policy YAML reference, and environment variable reference. Commit to docs/.

tasks.title: Group C - 7.7 - API reference docs (auto-generated from OpenAPI + narrative)
tasks.description: Generate API reference docs from the OpenAPI spec. Add narrative sections covering auth, pagination, error codes, and rate limits. Publish to docs/api/. Commit.

tasks.title: Group C - 7.8 - v1.0 release checklist + tag
tasks.description: Complete the v1.0 release checklist: all phase gates passed, security audit clean, accuracy CI green, docs complete, HIPAA checklist reviewed, CHANGELOG written, version tagged. Create the v1.0.0 git tag.

tasks.title: Post-1.0 - Native SMB/NFS protocol clients (deferred from v1.0)
tasks.description: Implement native SMB connector (smbprotocol) and NFS connector without requiring OS mounts. Deferred post-v1.0. Only OS-mount approach supported in v1.0.

tasks.title: Post-1.0 - Visual-layer PDF redaction (rasterize + OCR + overlay, deferred from v1.0)
tasks.description: Implement visual-layer PDF redaction: rasterize pages, run OCR to locate sensitive regions, apply black-box overlay, and re-flatten PDF. Deferred post-v1.0. Only text-layer redaction supported in v1.0.
